scenario: A team's weekly-report process has 5 versions deployed over 3 months. Operate's "Process" filter shows confusing data because instances from old versions are still completing. The team wants to filter dashboards to v5 only.
question: Which Operate filter fits?

options.text: Operate provides a version filter on the process list view that allows operators to select a specific process version and see only instances running on that version. Applying the version filter to show only v5 instances isolates the dashboard view to current deployments, filtering out older-version instances that are still completing. This is the built-in Operate feature for version-specific instance monitoring and is designed exactly for this post-deployment monitoring scenario.

Operate fully supports version filtering for process instances. The process list and instance search views include a version selector that narrows displayed instances to those running on a specific deployed version. Version filtering is a documented and commonly used Operate feature for monitoring multi-version deployments. Claiming this feature is absent would direct operators toward workarounds that the platform has solved natively.

Manually cancelling old-version instances forces all in-flight work on previous versions to terminate abruptly. Business transactions that are partially complete would be lost without compensation or rollback. This is appropriate only when old-version instances must be stopped -- for example, due to a critical bug fix. For simply filtering the monitoring view to show only v5 instances, using the Operate version filter achieves the goal without touching instance state at all.

Re-deploying v5 with a new processId creates an entirely separate process definition -- a different process, not a new version of the same process. All correlation keys, message subscriptions, and operational procedures configured for the original processId would need updating. Existing instances on the original processId would still appear in Operate under their original definition. Changing processId is not a version management strategy -- it is a separate process creation. ###

a.text: Correct. Operate's Filter panel on the Processes page supports selecting by process Name and Version (per process-instance-batch-modification.md:29: "in the Filter panel, select the Name and Version of the process you want to modify"; also seen at process-instance-migration.md:15 and delete-resources.md:21). Select v5 to see only those instances on the dashboards.

c.text: Incorrect. Cancelling running work to clean up dashboards is destructive.

d.text: Incorrect. Rename creates a new process, doesn't fix the filtering.

explanation: Operate's Filter panel on the Processes page supports selecting by process Name and Version (per process-instance-batch-modification.md:29: "in the Filter panel, select the Name and Version of the process you want to modify"; also seen at process-instance-migration.md:15 and delete-resources.md:21). Select v5 to see only those instances on the dashboards.
